text:
Admission Date:  2013-11-29     Discharge Date:  2007-12-31

Date of Birth:                  Sex:  F

Service:  MICU

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:  Ms. Johnson is a 33-year-old
female with an unclear past medical history admitted with
mental status changes.

The patient was evidently found in the lobby at Peterson Rehabilitation Hospital.  The exact details are unclear, but she was
apparently confused and agitated.  She may have exhibited
some seizure-like activity and was given 3 mg then 2 mg of
intravenous Ativan by Emergency Medical Service.

On arrival to the Emergency Department, she was confused and
required restraints and was intubated for airway protection.
Also on arrival, she was noted to have dilated pupils,
tachycardia, and a temperature of 102.  One of her
medications is amitriptyline, and her urine was positive for
TCA.  She was started on a bicarbonate drip and given
charcoal for a presumed TCA overdose.

Her husband reports that the patient's son has been
hospitalized at Wuesthoff Medical Center-rockledge for several weeks
following a renal transplant.  Additionally, he states his
wife has a history of lupus and fibromyalgia from which she
suffers from chronic pain.  This was all exacerbated six
months ago when she suffered a left knee injury.  For some
time now, she has been dependent on her MS Contin, Percocet,
and Lorazepam.  He states she frequently runs out of these
medications and calls multiple providers seeking refills.  He
states that two days ago, she ran out of MS Contin and
Percocet and was agitated because she could not get a refill.

On the prior to admission, her husband took all of her
medications and attempted to dispense them to her in the
manner prescribed.  When he awoke this morning, the
medications were gone.  On noticing this, he telephoned
Wuesthoff Medical Center-rockledge and was told by his son that his wife had
left the hospital to go meet him.  He knew this was not the
case and suspected something was wrong and immediately came
to the hospital to be with his son.  Jennifer Aguilar later received a
telephone call from the Emergency Room stating his wife was
at Administracion De Servicios Medicos De Pr (asem).

The husband denies that his wife any history of seizures or
syncope.  He is convinced this is from a drug overdose.  He
denies any recent infectious symptoms such as fever in his
wife.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  (All per husband)
1.  Lupus.
2.  Fibromyalgia.
3.  Hypothyroidism.
4.  Bipolar disorder.

MEDICATIONS ON ADMISSION:  (Per the husband and bottles he
had with him)
1.  Olanzapine 15 mg p.o. q.h.s.
2.  Levothyroxine 0.05 mg p.o. once per day.
3.  Amitriptyline 150 mg p.o. q.h.s.
4.  Lansoprazole 30 mg p.o. once per day.
5.  Lorazepam 0.5 mg p.o. as needed.
6.  Morphine sustained release 15 mg p.o. twice per day.
7.  Percocet as needed.

ALLERGIES:  Unknown.

FAMILY HISTORY:   The patient lives with her husband and her
two children in Jenny Peters.  Apparently, there is much stress
over their son's illness and Mrs. Johnson narcotic use.

SOCIAL HISTORY:  Per husband, a history of cocaine use,
intravenous drug abuse with heroin, a current smoker.  Her
husband denies alcohol; apparently she quit after a "bad
experience with cocaine and alcohol."

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION ON PRESENTATION:  Temperature was 99.2,
blood pressure was 140/90 and heart rate was 98.  In general,
she was sedated and intubated.  Head, eyes, ears, nose, and
throat examination revealed pupils were dilated.  The lungs
were clear.  Cardiac examination was unremarkable.  The
abdomen was benign.  The lower extremities revealed no
clubbing, cyanosis, or edema.  Skin revealed a tattoo on the
left lower leg.

PERTINENT LABORATORY VALUES ON PRESENTATION:  Laboratories on
admission were significant for a white blood cell count of
17.9.  A urine toxicology screen which was positive for
tricyclics.

HOSPITAL COURSE:   The patient was admitted to the Intensive
Care Unit and put on a bicarbonate drip for presumed TCA
overdose.  Electrocardiograms were checked q.12h.  Charcoal
50 g were given three times overnight.

A Psychiatry consultation was planned once the patient was
more alert.

On the second hospital day, her QRS intervals were normal.
All benzodiazepines and narcotics were held.  She was
extubated and saturating well.  Psychiatry was following to
consider possible hospitalization.

On hospital day three, she was awake and alert.
Consultations were obtained by Psychiatry and Social Work.
The psychiatric team came by on hospital day three and
advised that we discharge her to Orem Community Hospital HRI in 30266 Mitchell Estates
Cristinamouth, PR 05046, and
plans were made to do so.  She was sent there under a Section
12 for further evaluation and treatment.

CONDITION AT DISCHARGE:  Condition on discharge was stable.

DISCHARGE STATUS:  To Orem Community Hospital HRI.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES:  TCA overdose.

DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS/FOLLOWUP:   Followup will be
determined by the psychiatric team.


                    DR.Eric,Shannon 12-981



Dictated By:Mcdaniel
